presets.name: General Discovery
presets.description: Pure grounded theory — no seed categories, fully emergent taxonomy
presets.task_instruction: Analyze the following employee recognition messages and discover a taxonomy of recognition categories. Identify the key themes, behaviors, and values being recognized. Focus on emergent patterns from the data rather than applying predefined frameworks.
presets.additional_constraints: Aim for 5-10 top-level categories. Each should have 2-5 subcategories.

presets.name: Seeded Discovery
presets.description: Starts with common HR recognition categories as hints, but allows new ones to emerge
presets.task_instruction: Analyze the following employee recognition messages and build a taxonomy of recognition categories. Use the provided seed categories as starting points, but freely add new categories or discard seeds that aren't supported by the data.
presets.additional_constraints: Keep 5-8 top-level categories. Merge or split seeds as the data warrants.

presets.name: Mentorship Focus
presets.description: Narrow lens on coaching, professional development, and knowledge transfer patterns
presets.task_instruction: Analyze these recognition messages specifically for mentorship and professional development patterns. What coaching behaviors are being recognized? How do senior employees support junior ones? What knowledge transfer dynamics are visible?
presets.additional_constraints: Focus on developmental relationships. Limit to 4-7 categories. Include evidence strength (how many messages support each category).

presets.name: Cross-Functional Collaboration
presets.description: Focus on how teams recognize work that spans department boundaries
presets.task_instruction: Analyze these recognition messages for cross-functional and cross-departmental collaboration patterns. How do people recognize work that bridges organizational boundaries? What types of cross-team contributions get celebrated?
presets.additional_constraints: Pay special attention to nominator/recipient title differences as signals of cross-functional recognition. Limit to 4-7 categories.

presets.name: Values Alignment
presets.description: Map recognition messages to implicit company values — useful for culture audits
presets.task_instruction: Analyze these recognition messages to identify the core company values they reflect. Don't look for official value statements — instead discover what behaviors and qualities employees actually celebrate and reward each other for. These are the lived values of the organization.
presets.additional_constraints: Frame each category as a value (e.g., 'Ownership', 'Customer Obsession', 'Psychological Safety'). Aim for 5-8 values with concrete behavioral subcategories under each.
